deltas.releaseNoteText: Added `<velt-comments-sidebar-v2>` — a complete redesign of the Comments Sidebar built on a flat primitive component architecture. Every section of the UI is an independently importable and composable primitive. The V2 sidebar ships with a unified filter model (replacing the legacy `minimalFilter` + `advancedFilters` system), CDK virtual scroll for large comment lists, a focused-thread view, a minimal actions dropdown, and a filter dropdown.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting VeltCommentsSidebarV2 — the primitive-architecture redesign of the Comments Sidebar — covering its component props, wireframe customization tree, and the relationship to VeltCommentsSidebar version="2".

details.signature:
<VeltCommentsSidebarV2
  pageMode={boolean}
  focusedThreadMode={boolean}
  readOnly={boolean}
  embedMode={string | null}
  floatingMode={boolean}
  position={string}
  variant={string}
  forceClose={boolean}
  onSidebarOpen={(data) => void}
  onSidebarClose={(data) => void}
  onCommentClick={(data) => void}
  onCommentNavigationButtonClick={(data) => void}
/>

parameters.name: pageMode
parameters.description: Enable page-level comments mode.

parameters.name: focusedThreadMode
parameters.description: Open individual threads in a focused view inside the sidebar.

parameters.name: readOnly
parameters.description: Render the sidebar in read-only mode.

parameters.name: embedMode
parameters.description: Embed the sidebar inside a custom container.

parameters.name: floatingMode
parameters.description: Render the sidebar in floating mode.

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: Anchor position of the sidebar panel (e.g. "right").

parameters.name: variant
parameters.description: Display variant (e.g. "sidebar").

parameters.name: forceClose
parameters.description: Force the sidebar closed programmatically.

parameters.name: onSidebarOpen
parameters.description: Callback fired when the sidebar opens.

parameters.name: onSidebarClose
parameters.description: Callback fired when the sidebar closes.

parameters.name: onCommentClick
parameters.description: Callback fired when a comment item is clicked.

parameters.name: onCommentNavigationButtonClick
parameters.description: Callback fired when the comment navigation button is clicked.

deltas.releaseNoteText: `VeltCommentsSidebar` now accepts a `version` prop. Setting `version="2"` routes the existing `<velt-comments-sidebar>` tag to the full V2 implementation, forwarding all props (`pageMode`, `focusedThreadMode`, `readOnly`, `embedMode`, `floatingMode`, `position`, `variant`, `forceClose`).
deltas.summary: Add the new `version` prop to the VeltCommentsSidebar props table in the existing surface-sidebar rule, and add a usage note that version="2" routes to the full V2 implementation forwarding all existing props.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Set to "2" to route the existing velt-comments-sidebar tag to the full V2 primitive-architecture implementation, forwarding pageMode, focusedThreadMode, readOnly, embedMode, floatingMode, position, variant, and forceClose.

deltas.releaseNoteText: Added `additionalFields` to `CommentAnnotationDataProvider.config`. Unlike `fieldsToRemove` (which strips fields from Velt's DB), `additionalFields` are copied to your resolver endpoint but retained in Velt's storage — enabling data replication without removal.
deltas.summary: Update the existing data-comment-annotation-data-provider rule to add fieldsToRemove alongside the already-documented additionalFields, clarifying that additionalFields replicates fields to the resolver endpoint while retaining them in Velt storage, whereas fieldsToRemove strips fields from Velt's DB entirely.

parameters.name: config.additionalFields
parameters.description: Fields copied to your resolver endpoint payload but retained in Velt's storage. Use for data replication without removal.

parameters.name: config.fieldsToRemove
parameters.description: Fields stripped from Velt's DB before storage. Use for PII removal. Co-exists with additionalFields on the same config object.

deltas.releaseNoteText: Added `agentFields?: string[]` to [`CommentRequestQuery`](/api-reference/sdk/models/data-models#commentrequestquery) to filter comment count queries to annotations where `agent.agentFields` contains any of the provided values. When `agentFields` is set, the unread count query is skipped and treated as equal to the total count.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting the agentFields parameter on CommentRequestQuery, which filters getCommentAnnotationCount queries to annotations whose agent.agentFields array contains any of the provided values, and notes that unread count is treated as equal to total count when agentFields is set.

parameters.name: agentFields
parameters.description: Filters comment count queries to annotations where agent.agentFields contains any of the provided values. When set, the unread count query is skipped and treated as equal to the total count.

details.returnType: N/A — query object passed to getCommentAnnotationCount()

deltas.releaseNoteText: Added `notification` field on `VeltDataProvider` accepting a `NotificationDataProvider`. When configured, Velt calls your `get` and `delete` handlers to fetch and remove notification data from your own backend instead of Velt's storage. The resolution pipeline runs notification → user → comment, ensuring resolver-enriched user references are available when the user resolver runs. Only notifications with `notificationSource === 'custom'` are routed through the resolver. The [`Notification`](/api-reference/sdk/models/data-models#notification) model gains `isNotificationResolverUsed?: boolean` to detect resolver-enriched notifications downstream.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting NotificationDataProvider on VeltDataProvider — covering the get and delete handler signatures, NotificationResolverConfig options (resolveTimeout, getRetryConfig), the notification→user→comment resolution pipeline order, the notificationSource === 'custom' routing constraint, and the isNotificationResolverUsed field on the Notification model.

parameters.name: notification.get
parameters.description: Handler called by Velt to fetch notification PII from your backend. Receives organizationId and an array of notificationIds.

parameters.name: notification.delete
parameters.description: Handler called by Velt to delete a single notification from your backend. Receives notificationId and organizationId.

parameters.name: notification.config.resolveTimeout
parameters.description: Maximum milliseconds to wait for resolver response before timing out. Defaults to 30000.

parameters.name: notification.config.getRetryConfig
parameters.type: { retryCount: number; retryDelay: number; revertOnFailure: boolean }
parameters.description: Retry policy for the get handler: number of retries, delay between retries in ms, and whether to revert UI on final failure.

details.returnType: N/A — provider object passed to setDataProviders()

reason: Release v5.0.2-beta.9 contains no CRDT API or behavior changes. All changes are scoped to Comments (VeltCommentsSidebarV2, CommentAnnotationDataProvider.config, CommentRequestQuery.agentFields) and Notifications (NotificationDataProvider).
